title: Overflow checking should not be disabled for Enumerable.Sum
description: Overflow checking should not be disabled for "Enumerable.Sum"Analyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellerror-handlingWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoEnumerable.Sum()always executes addition in acheckedcontext, so anOverflowExceptionwill be thrown if the value exceedsMaxValue, even if anuncheckedcontext was specified. Therefore, using this method inside anuncheckedcontext will only make the code more confusing, since the behavior will still bechecked.This rule raises an issue when anuncheckedcontext is specified for aSumon integer types.ExceptionsWhen theSumcall is inside atry-catch block, no issues are reported, since the exception is properly handled.void Add(List<int> list) { unchecked { try { int total = list.Sum(); } catch (System.OverflowException e)
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects .Sum() calls inside any unchecked block, even if on separate lines, by tracking block state.
logic.triggering_conditions: Method declarations containing unchecked blocks with .Sum() calls inside (multi-line aware)
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes test methods, try-catch blocks, and logging contexts
never_trigger_on: Methods with try-catch blocks handling overflow
logic.reasoning: Detects .Sum() method calls inside unchecked blocks, even if the block and call are on separate lines. This ensures all noncompliant patterns are detected, not just single-line cases.

should_not_trigger: [Test] public void TestSum() { unchecked { int total = numbers.Sum(); } }
